_publications._publicationTitle: Longitudinal phenotype development in a minipig model of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: We performed an extensive CT-based skeletal measurement assessment in our matched cohort...We found statistically smaller long bones (femur, tibia, humerus, ulna, metacarpals) in the NF1+/ex42del minipigs compared to their wildtype siblings.
details: Used for extensive CT-based skeletal measurement assessment in matched cohort, enabling quantitative detection of statistically smaller long bones in NF1+/ex42del minipigs
